faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants or regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are larger than typical household ants (up to 5/8 inch) and are usually black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, rustling sounds inside walls, and seeing large ants especially at night. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests.

faqs.question: Are carpenter ants common in East Harlem buildings?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants are prevalent in East Harlem due to the abundance of older buildings with wooden structural elements, moisture issues from aging plumbing, and proximity to parks and trees. Pre-war walk-ups and brownstones are particularly susceptible, though newer buildings can also experience infestations.
